prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "俯卧 T 字" (prone T raise) exercise, view from above and slightly behind. Person posture: Athletic person doing a prone T: lying face-down on the floor, legs extended; both arms extended out to the sides at 90 degrees from the body so the body and arms form the letter T from above; thumbs up, palms facing the floor; arms lifted off the ground by squeezing the shoulder blades together; this works the middle trapezius and rhomboids; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: middle trapezius and rhomboids (scapular retraction). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: rear deltoid and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "中斜方肌" pointing to the middle trapezius; "菱形肌" pointing to the rhomboids; "三角肌后束 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the rear deltoid and erector spinae. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
